name: Creatine monohydrate
summary: Decision-ready summary: Creatine monohydrate is best framed for strength and power output; lean mass support; cognitive fatigue resistance; safety screen: advanced kidney disease; active renal monitoring concerns.
description: Creatine monohydrate supports strength; muscle; cognitive fatigue with high confidence in this merged decision layer.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.
contraindications: chronic kidney disease or reduced kidney function warrants clinician guidance due to increased creatinine load and unclear long-term safety in this population
NSAIDs or aminoglycoside antibiotics or other nephrotoxic medications due to a theoretical added burden on kidney function
dehydration or inadequate fluid intake during intense exercise or heat exposure due to creatine's water-retention effect in muscle tissue
bipolar disorder warrants clinician guidance due to case reports and a clinical-trial signal of increased manic-switch risk
lithium or other renally-cleared psychiatric medications due to altered fluid and electrolyte balance that can affect drug levels
high loading doses of 20 grams per day or more may increase gastrointestinal upset or bloating
